content.full_text:
February 22, 1977


Honorable Nancy B. Watson
Judge, Superior Court
Criminal Courts Building
210 West Temple
Los Angeles, California  90012

Dear Judge Watson:

This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of January 10
outlining the answers to questions you presented in your letters
to the Commission dated December 17 and December 21 concerning items
which must be reported by you on your Statement of Economic Interests.

Your letter of December 17 asked the following questions:

1.  Are you required to report an interest in real property
    located in Riverside County?

    Yes.  The jurisdiction of a Superior Court Judge is the
    State of California and, therefore, any real property
    held within the state must be reported.

2.  Are you required to report the income received from an
    irrevocable trust of which you are a trustee and
    beneficiary?

    You are required to report the investments, any other
    holdings and any income of the trust which would otherwise
    be reportable under the law.

3.  Are you required to report stocks received from a dividend
4.  reinvestment program, the monetary value of which is less
    than $1,500?  Are the shares received in this manner reportable?

    No.  You are only required to report investments of $1,000
    or more.  Dividends received from stocks registered with the
    Securities and Exchange Commission are not reportable as
    income.

Honorable Nancy D. Watson                    -2-                    February 22, 1977


5. Are you required to report income received from a rental
   apartment which is placed in a trust for a minor child?
   Neither you or the minor child have access to this income.

   No.

6. Are you required to report campaign funds raised on behalf
   of your husband who is an elected county official?

   No.

Your letter of December 21 asks whether or not you must report
gifts including an eight-day excursion on a chartered boat received
from a potential employer of your husband. All gifts received by
an elected official with a value of $25 or more must be reported.
You must estimate the value of the gifts received if the actual
value cannot be ascertained. You need only report the gifts
received by you and not those gifts received by your husband.

Please let me know if I or any other member of the Commission's
staff may be of additional assistance.

                                        Very truly yours,



                                        REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
                                        Technical Assistance Program Coordinator


RSX:mak

bcc: Jeanne P.

CHAMBERS OF

The Superior Court

FAMILY LAW DEPARTMENT
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012

December 17, 1976

NANCY B. WATSON
SUPERVISING JUDGE

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca 95804

Re: Statement of Economic Interests Form 721

Gentlemen:

Enclosed herewith please find a list of six
questions prefaced by particular fact situations
which relate to my situation.

Will you please respond in writing, in
sufficient time to permit me to make use of your
advice.

After January 1, 1977, my address will be
as follows:

Criminal Courts Building
210 West Temple
Los Angeles, Ca 90012

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation.

Yours very truly,

Nancy B. Watson

nbw/sc
encl.

I am a Judge of the Superior Court of Los Angeles
County.

(1) Am I required to report an interest in real property
located in Riverside County?

I am a trustee and beneficiary on an irrevocable inter-
vivos trust established many years ago by my parents as a
gift.

(2) Am I required to report the income received from this
trust? Am I required to list as an asset my proportionate
interest in the trust?

I am a trustee of a testamentary trust in which I have
no legal or beneficial interest.

(3) Am I required to report as assets all of the corpus
of the trust (stocks) standing in my name as trustee?

I own two stocks in which I have a dividend reinvest-
ment program, i.e., instead of receiving the dividends
I receive shares and/or fractional shares, the monetary
value of which is less than $150.

(4) Is the acquisition of the shares in this manner and
this amount required to be reported?

I have assigned to an adult non-dependent child as
guardian for a minor, dependent child, pursuant to the
Uniform Gifts to Minors Act, the rental ($75 per month)
received from an occupant of a garage apartment. Neither
I nor the minor child have access to this income.

(5) Am I required to report this income?

My husband is an elected county official. In August
a committee acting on his behalf held a fund-raising event.
He and the committee are required by law to file a cam-
paign statement with the County Registrar-Recorder pur-
suant to Government Code §§ 84200-84214, with which they
have complied.

(6) Am I required to report the proceeds of this event?
If so, may I do so by reference to the statement on file?

NANCY B. WATSON
SUPERVISING JUDGE

CHAMBERS OF

The Superior Court
FAMILY LAW DEPARTMENT
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012
December 21, 1976

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca 95804

Re: Statement of Economic Interests Form 721

Gentlemen:

In addition to the questions previously
submitted by me under date of December 17, 1976,
I am inquiring further as per the enclosed.

Again, please note my new address after
January 3, 1977:

Superior Court, Dept. 124
Criminal Courts Building
210 West Temple
Los Angeles, Ca 90012

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation.

Yours very truly,

Nancy B. Watson
Nancy B. Watson

nbw/sc
encl.

Nancy E. Watson

During 1976 my husband entered into a series of negotia-
tions with the Chairman of the Board and majority stockholder
of a nationwide corporation to assume the position of
President of that corporation. The corporation is head-
quartered in San Francisco, California, and has producing
facilities in seven cities in the United States from coast
to coast. The negotiations were initiated by a phone call
from the Chairman of the Board of the corporation on May 5,
1976.

My husband flew to San Francisco on May 14, 1976, and
discussed the offer with the Chairman of the Board and a
member of the Board of Directors. The member of the Board
of Directors is also President of a related company which
does business in the Western United States. During these
meetings, a series of oral agreements were reached with
respect to salary, stock options and certain fringe benefits.
Agreement was also reach that my husband would assume the
duties of President of the corporation on March 1, 1977.
The matter was then placed in the hands of attorneys for the
drafting of an agreement and negotiations continued through
the summer to finalize a contract. The Chairman of the
Board invited my husband and me to join him, along with the
President of the related company and member of the Board of
Directors and two attorneys who are outside counsel for the
corporate complexes and the house counsel for the corporation,
on a trip. We joined them on a chartered boat trip out of
Vancouver from September 4 to September 12, 1976, for the
purpose of finalizing some agreement with respect to my
husband's joining the corporation.

My husband and I flew to Vancouver on September 3 and
returned from Seattle on September 12, 1976. All expenses
were paid by the Chairman of the Board. Negotiations broke
down and a decision was reached by both sides to terminate
further negotiations. Within two weeks following September
12, 1976, another person was named President of that corpora-
tion.

The questions is, are the expenses for the trip report-
able under the Fair Political practices Act? If so, how and
in what manner should they be treated?

sections.conclusion: Real property in California must be reported regardless of county location. Trust income and holdings are reportable if otherwise reportable under law. Investments of $1,000 or more must be reported; dividend reinvestment shares valued under $150 need not be reported. Income from trusts where the judge has no access is not reportable. Campaign funds for a spouse need not be reported by the judge. All gifts of $25 or more must be reported, including the eight-day excursion; the judge need only report gifts received by herself, not those received by her husband.
sections.question_synthetic: Judge Nancy E. Watson seeks guidance on multiple reporting requirements under the Fair Political Practices Act regarding real property interests, trust assets and income, dividend reinvestment programs, income from trusts for minor children, campaign fundraising proceeds for her husband, and gifts including a chartered boat trip from a potential employer.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that Judge Watson must report California real property and reportable trust assets/income, but need not report small dividend reinvestments, inaccessible trust income, or spouse's campaign funds. All gifts valued at $25 or more must be reported, including the estimated value of the chartered boat trip, though only gifts received personally by the judge (not her husband) require reporting.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes incoming requests dated December 17 and December 21, 1976, followed by FPPC response dated February 22, 1977. OCR quality is moderate with some scanning artifacts. All six questions from December 17 letter and the gift question from December 21 letter are addressed in the response. Response is signed by Regina Siciliano-Kutchins, Technical Assistance Program Coordinator.

able under the Fair political practices Act?
If so, how and
in what manner should they be treated?

CONCLUSION: Real property in California must be reported regardless of county location. Trust income and holdings are reportable if otherwise reportable under law. Investments of $1,000 or more must be reported; dividend reinvestment shares valued under $150 need not be reported. Income from trusts where the judge has no access is not reportable. Campaign funds for a spouse need not be reported by the judge. All gifts of $25 or more must be reported, including the eight-day excursion; the judge need only report gifts received by herself, not those received by her husband.

Your letter of December 17 asked the following questions: 1. Are you required to report an interest in real property located in Riverside County? Yes. The jurisdiction of a Superior Court Judge is the State of California and, therefore, any real property held within the state must be reported. 2. Are you required to report the income received from an irrevocable trust of which you are a trustee and beneficiary? You are required to report the investments, any other holdings and any income of the trust which would otherwise be reportable under the law. 3. No. You are only required to report investments of $1,000 or more. Dividends received from stocks registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission are not reportable as income. Neither you or the minor child have access to this income. No. 6. Are you required to report campaign funds raised on behalf of your husband who is an elected county official? embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Judge Nancy E. Watson's inquiries regarding economic interest disclosure requirements, providing detailed guidance on reporting obligations for real property, trusts, dividend programs, campaign funds, and gifts received from a potential employer.
